disclaimer: Seed data compiled from public sources. Every row confirmed_by_counsel=false until ratified by immigration counsel. Not legal advice.

rules.source_citation: Boundless mirror of USCIS Processing Times tool (egov.uscis.gov/processing-times), snapshot 2026-02-11
rules.notes: UNVERIFIED: not confirmable against the official egov.uscis.gov tool (UI and API returned 403 on 2026-07-06); value re-confirmed 2026-07-06 only against the Boundless mirror, whose page still shows the 'Updated February 11, 2026' snapshot. OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. All-I-129 regular-processing national median as of 2026-02-11. Applies equally to h1b_extension_pending and h1b_amendment_pending.

rules.value: 2.0-5.0 months by service center: CSC 3.5-5.0; NSC 2.0-4.0; TSC 3.0-4.5; VSC 2.5-4.5
rules.source_citation: USVisaStack mirror of USCIS Processing Times tool (egov.uscis.gov/processing-times), data updated March 2026, published April 2026
rules.notes: UNVERIFIED: not confirmable against the official egov.uscis.gov tool (403-blocked on 2026-07-06); per-center ranges re-confirmed 2026-07-06 against usvisastack.ai (page dated April 2026, data date March 2026). OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. Range format is median-to-93rd-percentile per USCIS convention. Applies equally to h1b_extension_pending and h1b_amendment_pending. CONFLICT: manifestlaw.com (June 2026) reports 8-10.5 months for most H-1B I-129 petitions (re-confirmed via search 2026-07-06); could not reconcile against the official tool (blocked). Verify on egov.uscis.gov before relying.

Legal guarantee, not a snapshot: USCIS must take adjudicative action within 15 BUSINESS days of receiving I-907 for I-129 H-1B. An RFE/NOID stops and resets the clock. In force as of 2026-07-06; no litigation identified. Corroborated 2026-07-06 by FR Doc 2026-00321 fn.12 (action within 15/30/45 days per 8 CFR 106.4(e)-(f)) and multiple 2026 practitioner sources; uscis.gov premium page itself 403-blocked.

rules.source_citation: Boundless mirror of USCIS data, snapshot 2026-02-11
rules.notes: UNVERIFIED: not confirmable against the official egov.uscis.gov tool (403-blocked on 2026-07-06); re-confirmed 2026-07-06 only against the Boundless mirror ('I-129 (Premium): 12 days'). OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. Actual median calendar days to action on premium I-129, faster than the 15-business-day legal ceiling.

rules.source_citation: Prior fee recited in FR Doc. 2026-00321 (Adjustment to Premium Processing Fees, Jan 12, 2026)
rules.notes: Superseded. I-907 fee for I-129 requesting E-1/E-2/E-3/H-1B/H-3/L-1A/L-1B/LZ/O/P/Q-1/TN classifications, in effect 2024-02-26 through 2026-02-28. Old fee ($2,805) and 2024-02-26 effective date confirmed 2026-07-06 against the FR Doc 2026-00321 Public Inspection PDF (primary).

rules.source_citation: FR Doc. 2026-00321, Adjustment to Premium Processing Fees (Jan 12, 2026); CPI-U inflation adjustment under USCIS Stabilization Act
rules.notes: In force as of 2026-07-06. Applies to I-907 POSTMARKED on or after 2026-03-01 for I-129 H-1B (and E-1/E-2/E-3/H-3/L-1A/L-1B/LZ/O-1/O-2/P-1/P-1S/P-2/P-2S/P-3/P-3S/Q-1/TN-1/TN-2). Wrong-fee filings are rejected. CONFIRMED 2026-07-06 against the FR Doc 2026-00321 Public Inspection PDF (primary): $2,805 to $2,965, CPI-U +5.72% June 2023-June 2025, effective/postmark date March 1, 2026. Also corroborated by Ogletree, Penn Global, Boundless alerts. No litigation identified against this rule.

rules.source_citation: Boundless mirror of USCIS Processing Times tool, snapshot 2026-02-11
rules.notes: UNVERIFIED: not confirmable against the official egov.uscis.gov tool (403-blocked on 2026-07-06); re-confirmed 2026-07-06 only against the Boundless mirror (8.1 months regular; page also shows I-140 premium actual median 21 days). OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. All-I-140 regular-processing national median as of 2026-02-11.

rules.value: 3-17 months depending on center and category; EB-1A 3-6; EB-2 NIW 4-7; EB-2 advanced degree and EB-3 skilled worker 8-17
rules.source_citation: USVisaStack mirror of USCIS Processing Times tool, data updated March-April 2026
rules.notes: UNVERIFIED: not confirmable against the official egov.uscis.gov tool (403-blocked on 2026-07-06); re-confirmed 2026-07-06 against usvisastack.ai (EB-1A: NSC 3.0-5.0, TSC 4.0-6.0; EB-2 NIW: NSC 4.0-6.0, TSC 5.0-7.0; EB-2 advanced degree: NSC 9.0-17.0, TSC 8.0-15.0; EB-3 skilled: NSC 9.0-17.0, TSC 8.0-15.0). CORRECTED 2026-07-06: prior seed said EB-2 advanced degree/EB-3 span '4-17'; source shows 8-17. Caution: the same tracker page also lists conflicting duplicate NIW rows of 26-38 months, so treat as low-reliability. OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. NSC and TSC breakdowns; ranges are median-to-93rd-percentile. Verify on egov.uscis.gov before relying.

Legal guarantee for I-140 EB-1 (E11/E12) and EB-2/EB-3 (E21 non-NIW, E31/E32/EW-3): 15 BUSINESS days. See separate row for NIW/E13 45-business-day track. Corroborated 2026-07-06 by FR Doc 2026-00321 fn.12 (action within 15/30/45 days per 8 CFR 106.4(e)-(f)); uscis.gov premium page itself 403-blocked.

rules.source_citation: 8 CFR 106.4(f); USCIS premium processing expansion (2022)
rules.notes: 45 BUSINESS days for I-140 E21 NIW and E13 multinational executive/manager premium processing. VERIFIED 2026-07-06: FR Doc 2026-00321 fn.12 confirms the 15/30/45-day structure under 8 CFR 106.4(e)-(f), and multiple 2026 sources (tukki.ai, tryalma.com, manifestlaw.com) confirm 45 business days for E13/E21-NIW specifically. FR Doc 2026-00321 also confirms E13 and E21 NIW premium eligibility for all filings as of 2023-01-30; phased availability began 2022-06-01 for previously filed petitions.

rules.source_citation: Prior fee recited in FR Doc. 2026-00321
rules.notes: Superseded. I-907 fee for I-140 (E11/E12/E13/E21/E31/E32/EW-3), in effect 2024-02-26 through 2026-02-28. Old fee ($2,805) and effective date confirmed 2026-07-06 against the FR Doc 2026-00321 Public Inspection PDF (primary).

rules.source_citation: FR Doc. 2026-00321, Adjustment to Premium Processing Fees (Jan 12, 2026)
rules.notes: In force as of 2026-07-06. Applies to I-907 for I-140 (E11/E12/E13/E21/E31/E32/EW-3) postmarked on or after 2026-03-01. CONFIRMED 2026-07-06 against the FR Doc 2026-00321 Public Inspection PDF (primary): 'The fee for all Form I-140 petitioners requesting premium processing will increase from $2,805 to $2,965.' No litigation identified against this rule.

rules.source_citation: Boundless mirror of USCIS Processing Times tool, snapshot 2026-02-11
rules.notes: UNVERIFIED: not confirmable against the official egov.uscis.gov tool (403-blocked on 2026-07-06); re-confirmed 2026-07-06 only against the Boundless mirror (7 months, snapshot 2026-02-11). OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. Employment-based I-485 median as of 2026-02-11.

rules.source_citation: ImmigrationDirect (June 2026) citing USCIS historical processing-times data
rules.notes: UNVERIFIED: not confirmable against the official egov.uscis.gov tool (403-blocked on 2026-07-06); re-confirmed 2026-07-06 against ImmigrationDirect (page last updated 2026-06-08: EB median 5.7, field offices 9.0, SCOPS 35.5, 'most I-485 take approximately 8 to 14 months') and USVisaStack (Mar 2026: NBC EB adjustment 8.5-14.5, EB overall 8.0-24.0). OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. Sources conflict on the headline number (5.7 vs 7.0 Boundless Feb 2026 vs 8.5-14.5 NBC) - the 5.7 likely reflects a different completion cohort; verify on egov.uscis.gov. Clock effectively pauses while priority date is not current on the Visa Bulletin. Context: Dorcas Int'l Inst. of RI v. USCIS (D.R.I. 2026-06-05) vacated four USCIS adjudication-hold policies, which may shift EB I-485/EAD medians in later snapshots; it does not change any value here.

rules.source_citation: 8 CFR 106.4 (I-485 not a designated premium-eligible benefit)
rules.notes: No premium processing exists for I-485 as of 2026-07-06. Corroborated 2026-07-06 by FR Doc 2026-00321 (primary), which lists the premium-eligible populations as Forms I-129, I-140, I-539, and I-765 only.

rules.source_citation: Boundless mirror of USCIS Processing Times tool, snapshot 2026-02-11
rules.notes: UNVERIFIED: not confirmable against the official egov.uscis.gov tool (403-blocked on 2026-07-06); re-confirmed 2026-07-06 only against the Boundless mirror (1.9 months; a separate Boundless entry shows 0.6 months, underscoring low reliability). OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. Aggregate median across ALL I-765 categories - NOT OPT-specific; do not use for F-1 planning. USCIS publishes c3 figures as a single (c)(3) bucket, not per c3A/c3B/c3C.

rules.source_citation: USVisaStack mirror of USCIS Processing Times tool, data updated March-April 2026
rules.notes: UNVERIFIED: not confirmable against the official egov.uscis.gov tool (403-blocked on 2026-07-06); the ~4.0 median is a tracker estimate (USCIS does not publish per-c3A figures). Range 3-5 months re-confirmed 2026-07-06 against usvisastack.ai (April 2026 data). OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. Pre-completion OPT (c)(3)(A). USCIS reports c3 categories as one bucket.

rules.source_citation: USVisaStack mirror of USCIS Processing Times tool, data updated March-April 2026; corroborated by TrackMyOPT (March 2026 USCIS data)
rules.notes: UNVERIFIED: not confirmable against the official egov.uscis.gov tool (403-blocked on 2026-07-06); the ~4.0 median is a tracker estimate (USCIS does not publish per-c3B figures). Range 3-5 months re-confirmed 2026-07-06 against usvisastack.ai (April 2026 data); usvisastack.ai does not quantify online vs paper, so the online 2-3 / paper 3-5 / RFE 6-9 split rests on TrackMyOPT (March 2026) alone, not re-verified in this pass. OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. Post-completion OPT (c)(3)(B).

rules.source_citation: USVisaStack mirror of USCIS Processing Times tool, data updated March-April 2026
rules.notes: UNVERIFIED: not confirmable against the official egov.uscis.gov tool (403-blocked on 2026-07-06); the ~4.5 median is a tracker estimate (USCIS does not publish per-c3C figures). Range 3-6 months re-confirmed 2026-07-06 against usvisastack.ai (April 2026 data). OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. STEM OPT extension (c)(3)(C). Timely-filed STEM extension gives automatic 180-day EAD extension while pending (separate rules domain).

rules.source_citation: USVisaStack mirror of USCIS Processing Times tool, data updated March-April 2026
rules.notes: UNVERIFIED: not confirmable against the official egov.uscis.gov tool (403-blocked on 2026-07-06); the ~3.0 median is a tracker estimate. Range 2-4 months re-confirmed 2026-07-06 against usvisastack.ai (April 2026 data). OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. Adjustment-based EAD (c)(9). Premium processing NOT available for c9 (FR Doc 2026-00321 limits I-765 premium eligibility to OPT/STEM-OPT categories).

rules.source_citation: 8 CFR 106.4(f); USCIS premium processing expansion to F-1 I-765 (2023)
rules.notes: Legal guarantee: 30 BUSINESS days for I-765 categories c3A, c3B, c3C (pre-completion OPT, post-completion OPT, STEM OPT). VERIFIED 2026-07-06: FR Doc 2026-00321 (primary) confirms USCIS began I-765 OPT/STEM premium in March 2023 - premium upgrade requests for pending F-1 I-765s from 2023-03-06, and ALL pending and initial F-1 c3 filings from 2023-04-03; fn.12 confirms the 15/30/45-day structure under 8 CFR 106.4(e)-(f). 30-business-day clock also confirmed by usvisastack.ai, TrackMyOPT, and Immiva (2026).

rules.source_citation: Prior fee recited in FR Doc. 2026-00321
rules.notes: Superseded. I-907 fee for I-765 OPT/STEM OPT categories, in effect 2024-02-26 through 2026-02-28. Old fee ($1,685) and effective date confirmed 2026-07-06 against the FR Doc 2026-00321 Public Inspection PDF (primary).

rules.source_citation: FR Doc. 2026-00321, Adjustment to Premium Processing Fees (Jan 12, 2026)
rules.notes: In force as of 2026-07-06. Applies to I-907 for I-765 OPT/STEM OPT (c3 categories) postmarked on or after 2026-03-01. CONFIRMED 2026-07-06 against the FR Doc 2026-00321 Public Inspection PDF (primary): I-765 premium $1,685 to $1,780. Same rule raised I-539 F-1/F-2/J-1/J-2/M-1/M-2 premium fee $1,965 to $2,075 and I-129 H-2B/R-1 fee $1,685 to $1,780 (both also confirmed against the PDF). No litigation identified against this rule.

rules.source_citation: Boundless mirror of USCIS Processing Times tool, snapshot 2026-02-11
rules.notes: UNVERIFIED: not confirmable against the official egov.uscis.gov tool (403-blocked on 2026-07-06); re-confirmed 2026-07-06 only against the Boundless mirror (AP 6.1, travel document 6.4). OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. I-131 advance parole median 6.1 months; general travel document 6.4 months, as of 2026-02-11.

rules.source_citation: USVisaStack mirror of USCIS Processing Times tool, March 2026 data
rules.notes: UNVERIFIED: not confirmable against the official egov.uscis.gov tool (403-blocked on 2026-07-06); re-confirmed 2026-07-06 against usvisastack.ai (March 2026 data: AP 5.0-7.0 NBC). OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. I-131 advance parole for pending I-485, NBC; often adjudicated concurrently with I-485. No premium processing for I-131.

rules.source_citation: USVisaStack mirror of USCIS Processing Times tool, March 2026 data
rules.notes: UNVERIFIED: not confirmable against the official egov.uscis.gov tool (403-blocked on 2026-07-06); re-confirmed 2026-07-06 against usvisastack.ai (March 2026 data: reentry permit 9.0-12.0, refugee travel document 4.0-6.0). OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. I-131 reentry permit for LPRs; refugee travel documents run 4.0-6.0 months per the same snapshot.

rules.source_citation: 8 CFR 106.4 (I-131 not a designated premium-eligible benefit)
rules.notes: No premium processing exists for I-131 as of 2026-07-06. Corroborated 2026-07-06 by FR Doc 2026-00321 (primary, premium-eligible forms are I-129/I-140/I-539/I-765 only) and usvisastack.ai ('Form I-907 is not available for any I-131 category').

rules.source_citation: DOL OFLC FLAG Processing Times, as of 6/30/2026
rules.notes: OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. PRIMARY SOURCE, fetched directly 2026-07-06. OFLC analyst review queue is working ETA-9089 applications filed in June 2025 (month granularity). OFLC may complete some applications filed before the posted month.

rules.source_citation: DOL OFLC FLAG Processing Times, as of 6/30/2026 (June 2026 determinations)
rules.notes: OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. PRIMARY SOURCE. Average calendar days for PERM analyst-review determinations issued in June 2026 (~13.2 months).

rules.source_citation: DOL OFLC FLAG Processing Times, as of 6/30/2026
rules.notes: OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. PRIMARY SOURCE. Audit review queue working cases from December 2025; note audit queue date is measured from original filing per OFLC convention.

rules.source_citation: DOL OFLC FLAG Processing Times, as of 6/30/2026 (June 2026 determinations)
rules.notes: OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. PRIMARY SOURCE. Average calendar days for audit-review determinations issued in June 2026.

rules.notes: OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. PRIMARY SOURCE. Requests for reconsideration to the Certifying Officer: queue working requests from February 2026.

rules.source_citation: DOL OFLC FLAG Processing Times, as of 6/30/2026
rules.notes: OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. PRIMARY SOURCE. NPWC is issuing OEWS-based H-1B prevailing wage determinations (ETA-9141) for requests received April 2026 (~2-3 month turnaround).

rules.source_citation: DOL OFLC FLAG Processing Times, as of 6/30/2026
rules.notes: OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. PRIMARY SOURCE. OEWS-based PERM PWDs being issued for requests received April 2026. PWD is a pre-filing PERM step; keyed to perm_filed as the closest canonical status.

rules.notes: OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. PRIMARY SOURCE. Non-OEWS (employer-provided survey) H-1B PWDs being issued for requests received March 2026.

rules.notes: OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. PRIMARY SOURCE. Non-OEWS (employer-provided survey) PERM PWDs being issued for requests received March 2026.

rules.notes: OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. PRIMARY SOURCE. PWD redetermination requests (H-1B and PERM) being processed for requests received April 2026.

rules.source_citation: DOL OFLC FLAG Processing Times, as of 6/30/2026
rules.notes: OPERATIONAL SNAPSHOT (volatile), not a legal rule. PRIMARY SOURCE. PERM Center Director reviews of PWDs being processed for requests received May 2026.

rules.source_citation: USCIS Processing Times tool methodology (median of completions in prior 6 months, refreshed monthly)
rules.notes: UNVERIFIED: egov.uscis.gov/processing-times/more-info returned 403 on 2026-07-06, so the monthly cadence and median-of-completions methodology rest on stable prior knowledge, not an in-session fetch of the official page. USCIS refreshes published processing times roughly monthly; figures are medians of recently completed cases, so they lag current intake. Applies to all USCIS snapshot rows in this file.

rules.source_citation: DOL OFLC FLAG Processing Times page (posted monthly, 'as of' month-end)
rules.notes: OFLC posts PERM and PWD queue snapshots monthly; current snapshot is as of 6/30/2026. Applies to all FLAG snapshot rows in this file.
